keyPoints: Longitudinal, multi-wave design with randomized or quasi-experimental AI exposure arms to track change in nonverbal skills and empathy.
Triangulate validated self-report empathy scales (IRI, EQ, QCAE) with objective nonverbal-behavior measures (NVS, RMET, gaze, posture, gesture, synchrony via MEA/OpenPose).
Incorporate behavioral assessments during AI and human interactions, coded video and simulated-conversation tasks to elicit measurable nonverbal responses.
Test mediation, especially whether perceived “feeling heard” and perceived listening quality mediate AI exposure effects on empathy and nonverbal calibration.
Assess mechanisms like nonverbal synchrony dynamics (cross-wavelet coherence) and potential negative spillovers (narrowed expressive repertoires, overreliance).
Embed ethical safeguards and governance: privacy, consent, data security, pre-registration; transparency about platform heterogeneity.
